metadata.title: The Dilemma of Choice: How Design Shapes Consumer Decisions
metadata.description: Acharya Abstract, Prashant S. (2025): The Dilemma of Choice: How Design Shapes Consumer Decisions. FishTaxa 36 (9): 422-429
references: 1. Brignull, H. (2010). Deceptive design: Types of dark patterns. https://www.deceptive.design/types
2. Caragay, E., Xiong, K., Zong, J., & Jackson, D. (2023). Beyond dark patterns: A concept-based framework for software design ethics. https://groups.csail.mit.edu/sdg/pubs/2023/dark-concepts-23.pdf
3. Dayananda, L. (2025). Dark patterns in user experience design: The erosion of user autonomy and trust. https://www.researchgate.net/publication/391156550
4. Di Geronimo, L., Braz, L., Faria, A., et al. (2020). UI dark patterns and where to find them. Proceedings of the CHI 2020 Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems.
5. Gray, C. M., Kou, Y., Battles, B., Hoggatt, J., & Toombs, A. L. (2018). The dark (patterns) side of UX design. Proceedings of the 2018 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems.

6. Gunawan, J., Fitri, N., & Wibowo, P. (2021). A comparative study of dark patterns across mobile and web modalities. https://www.ftc.gov/system/files/documents/public_events/1582978/gunawan_pradeep_choffnes_hartzog_wilson_dark_patte
7. Kollmer, T. (2022). Dark patterns: Ethical and economic perspectives. Business & Information Systems Engineering. https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12599-022-00783-7
8. Mathur, A., Acar, G., Friedman, M. G., et al. (2019). Dark patterns at scale: Findings from a crawl of 11,000 shopping websites. Proceedings of the ACM on Human-Computer Interaction, 3(CSCW).
9. Nielsen Norman Group. (2023). Deceptive patterns: When persuasive design becomes deception. https://www.nngroup.com/articles/deceptive-patterns/
10. Raw Studio. (2023). Designing with integrity: The ethical designer's handbook on dark patterns. https://raw.studio/blog/designing-with-integrity-the-ethical-designers-handbook-on-dark-patterns/
11. Thaler, R. H., & Sunstein, C. R. (2008). Nudge: Improving decisions about health, wealth, and happiness. Yale University Press.
12. Think Design. (2021). Responsible design - Bait and switch. https://think.design/blog/responsible-design-part-6-of-14-bait-and-switch/
13. UITOP. (2023). Dark patterns in UX: What they are and how to avoid them. https://uitop.design/blog/design/dark-patterns-in-ux/
14. UX Design. (2022). Little UX crimes: A collection of dark patterns in design. https://uxdesign.cc/little-ux-crimes-a-collectionof-dark-patterns-in-design-7783b75195e0
15. UX Design Institute. (2023). What are dark patterns in UX? https://www.uxdesigninstitute.com/blog/what-are-dark-patternsin-ux/
16. Zero To Mastery. (2023). Dark patterns in UX: How they trick users (and how to avoid them). https://zerotomastery.io/blog/dark-patterns-in-ux/

metadata.title: PUDL Raw FERC Form 714
metadata.description:
Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) Form 714 Data, archived from https://www.ferc.gov/industries-data/electric/general-information/electric-industry-forms/form-no-714-annual-electric/data

This archive contains raw input data for the Public Utility Data Liberation (PUDL) software developed by Catalyst Cooperative. It is organized into Frictionless Data Packages. For additional information about this data and PUDL, see the following resources:

  • The PUDL Repository on GitHub
  • PUDL Documentation
  • Other Catalyst Cooperative data archives

metadata.title: PUDL Raw Mine Safety and Health Administration (MSHA) Mines
metadata.description:
The Mine dataset lists all Coal and Metal/Non-Metal mines under MSHA's jurisdiction. It includes such information as the current status of each mine (Active, Abandoned, NonProducing, etc.), the current owner and operating company, commodity codes and physical attributes of the mine. Archived from https://arlweb.msha.gov/OpenGovernmentData/OGIMSHA.asp

This archive contains raw input data for the Public Utility Data Liberation (PUDL) software developed by Catalyst Cooperative. It is organized into Frictionless Data Packages. For additional information about this data and PUDL, see the following resources:

  • The PUDL Repository on GitHub
  • PUDL Documentation
  • Other Catalyst Cooperative data archives

metadata.title: EPA MATS -- Mercury and Air Toxics Standards
metadata.description:
The EPA Mercury and Air Toxics Standards (MATS) dataset provides detailed information on emissions of hazardous air pollutants, specifically mercury and other toxic substances, from power plants in the United States. Established by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) under the Clean Air Act, MATS aims to reduce air pollution and its associated health risks by setting limits on the emissions of mercury, arsenic, acid gases, and other pollutants from coal- and oil-fired power plants. The dataset typically includes metrics such as emission levels, compliance testing results, facility locations, and operational data. It serves as a critical resource for policymakers, researchers, and environmental organizations to assess the effectiveness of pollution control measures, monitor compliance, and evaluate the environmental and public health impacts of power plant emissions. Archived from https://www.epa.gov/stationary-sources-air-pollution/mercury-and-air-toxics-standards

This archive contains raw input data for the Public Utility Data Liberation (PUDL) software developed by Catalyst Cooperative. It is organized into Frictionless Data Packages. For additional information about this data and PUDL, see the following resources:

  • The PUDL Repository on GitHub
  • PUDL Documentation
  • Other Catalyst Cooperative data archives

metadata.title: PUDL Raw EPA Hourly Continuous Emission Monitoring System (CEMS)
metadata.description:
US EPA hourly Continuous Emissions Monitoring System (CEMS) data. Hourly CO2, SO2, NOx emissions and gross load. Archived from https://campd.epa.gov/

This archive contains raw input data for the Public Utility Data Liberation (PUDL) software developed by Catalyst Cooperative. It is organized into Frictionless Data Packages. For additional information about this data and PUDL, see the following resources:

  • The PUDL Repository on GitHub
  • PUDL Documentation
  • Other Catalyst Cooperative data archives

In order to enable more regular CEMS updates, this dataset replaces a prior PUDL EPA CEMS archive that pulled one annual file for each state. That archive is no longer updated but can be found here.

data.title: PUDL Raw EPA Hourly Continuous Emission Monitoring System (CEMS)

metadata.title: PUDL Raw EIA Form 930 -- Hourly and Daily Balancing Authority Operations Report
metadata.description:
The EIA Form 930 provides hourly demand and generation statistics by balancing area (or sub-balancing area in the case of some larger areas). These statistics include a breakdown by energy source (coal, gas, hydro, wind, solar, etc.) as well as interchange between the balancing areas, including international exchanges with Canada and Mexico. Archived from https://www.eia.gov/electricity/gridmonitor/

This archive contains raw input data for the Public Utility Data Liberation (PUDL) software developed by Catalyst Cooperative. It is organized into Frictionless Data Packages. For additional information about this data and PUDL, see the following resources:

  • The PUDL Repository on GitHub
  • PUDL Documentation
  • Other Catalyst Cooperative data archives
